<!-- Generated by `npm run build:context` (GRAMMAR.md §10). Do not edit by hand. -->

# Border utilities

Utilities are token-bound class grammar, not hard-coded values. They live in `@layer utilities`, so unlayered consumer CSS overrides them without `!important`.

- Pattern families use `base[-edge]-{value}`. Edges are logical (`block`, `inline`, `block-start`, `inline-end`) unless a family says otherwise.
- When a family lists responsive support, prefix its class with `tablet:`, `tablet-lg:`, `desktop:`, `desktop-lg:`, or `widescreen:`.
- Negative values use `neg-{value}` only where the family offers them.
- Values resolve through design tokens; use the documented grammar instead of writing raw CSS values.

## Border utilities

Border width, radius and style utilities. Width and radius bind to the border token scale per side/corner; the values are emitted verbatim because the border tokens are keyed width-<size>/radius-<size> rather than <size>.

### border-width

Sets border width on all sides or a specific side/axis, from the border-width token scale.

Grammar: `border-width[-edge]-{value}` — edges: y, x, top, bottom, left, right · values: 0, xs, sm, md, lg, xl · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

- Border width alone does not draw a border — pair with a border-style utility (border-solid) and a border color.

### radius

Sets border radius on all corners or a specific corner/edge, from the border-radius token scale. radius-pill is a fully rounded (99rem) value.

Grammar: `radius[-edge]-{value}` — edges: top, bottom, left, right, top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right · values: 0, xs, sm, md, lg, xl, pill · responsive: prefix with `tablet:` `tablet-lg:` `desktop:` `desktop-lg:` `widescreen:`

### border-style

Sets the border line style.

Classes: `border-solid`, `border-dashed`, `border-dotted`.
Sets: `border-solid`: border-style; `border-dashed`: border-style; `border-dotted`: border-style.
